title: campanula scheuchzeri subf. styriaca
intro: Campanula scheuchzeri subf. styriaca, also known as alpine bellflower, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the campanula family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing fever and soothing respiratory issues. Alpine bellflower has also been used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and ulcers, due to its supposed ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Campanula scheuchzeri subf. styriaca include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
